steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has two facts about the documentary: what it's about and who directed it. The main verb is 'was directed,' so the subject clause needs to be subordinate. A nonrestrictive relative clause ('which' with a comma before it) correctly makes the content description subordinate.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'no relative pronoun, comma before 'was directed'': without a subordinating word, the sentence splits into two independent clauses joined only by a comma — a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — 'documentary which' (no comma before 'which')': omitting the comma before 'which' turns the clause restrictive, implying there are multiple documentaries and this construction is needed to identify the correct one — not the intent here. ✗
  • Choice C — 'documentary, which... horses': if the clause ends without a closing comma before 'was directed,' the nonrestrictive clause is improperly punctuated, leaving 'was directed by Cherokee filmmaker Neta Rhyne' dangling as a fragment. ✗
  • Choice D — 'documentary, which... horses, was directed': the nonrestrictive clause is correctly set off by commas on both sides, and 'was directed by Cherokee filmmaker Neta Rhyne' serves as the proper main verb. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
